source_evidence: R250 evidence map: Claw-ED README.md:5, 30-45, 55-60, 78 and generate_lesson_bundle.py:173-205 describe complete lesson bundles, teacher DOCX, student DOCX, PPTX, differentiated outputs, and multi-format export.
problem_it_solves: A prep system that only produces a lesson body does not give the teacher enough classroom-ready artifacts.
input: Accepted single_lesson_template, teacher review status, classroom_timeline, big_screen_page_plan, source ledger.
output_artifact: lesson_bundle_manifest listing teacher guide, student worksheet, slide storyboard, rubric, differentiation support, classroom timeline, and review notes.
stage_owner: Future R95 export readiness, after R230/R252 contracts exist.
human_review_point: Teacher must approve bundle manifest before any formal export is created.
do_not_adopt: Do not copy Claw-ED runtime, export code, file writers, provider calls, browser, scheduler, Telegram, or Google Drive tools.
risk: Opening bundle export too early can bypass source policy and teacher review.
preconditions_before_use: R201/R220 stable, R201M teacher review gate mature, R230 big-screen contract present, R252 classroom timeline contract present.
acceptance_test_for_future: Given an accepted template and timeline, manifest lists every artifact with source, dependency, review status, and export eligibility; no file export occurs.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: generate_lesson_bundle.py:173-205 compiles teacher, student, and slideshow views from a shared master content object.
problem_it_solves: Generated artifacts can drift if each output is produced independently.
input: Accepted lesson design, classroom timeline, source ledger, teacher notes, artifact manifest.
output_artifact: artifact_dependency_graph showing which artifact depends on lesson body, timeline, source material, teacher confirmation, or accepted model patch.
stage_owner: Future R95 planning with R252/R230 dependencies.
human_review_point: Teacher sees dependency warnings before accepting export-ready artifacts.
do_not_adopt: Do not adopt Claw-ED's shared content model or compile functions.
risk: Without dependency graph, slide/worksheet/rubric may cite content not accepted in teacher main text.
preconditions_before_use: Artifact IDs and source statuses must be stable across single_lesson_template, timeline, and big-screen plan.
acceptance_test_for_future: Changing a source-backed lesson section marks dependent worksheet, slide, rubric, and timeline nodes as stale until regenerated or reapproved.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: Claw-ED README.md:55 and 90 describe quality gate checks such as Bloom progression, stimulus-based assessment, differentiation specificity, diversity audit, and auto-retry.
problem_it_solves: Lesson artifacts need quality checks beyond schema validity.
input: Candidate artifact, source ledger, lesson objectives, classroom timeline, teacher-review state.
output_artifact: quality_gate_checklist with source grounding, objective alignment, assessment fit, differentiation specificity, pacing feasibility, teacher readability, and export readiness.
stage_owner: Future quality sentinel before R95 export and before accepted-preview promotion.
human_review_point: Teacher sees failed checks and must decide accept, reject, or defer candidate changes.
do_not_adopt: Do not adopt Claw-ED auto-retry behavior into current route; model retries remain separate candidate generation, not baseline mutation.
risk: Auto-retry can silently rewrite teacher main text if not bound to review gate.
preconditions_before_use: Teacher review states and source confidence statuses must be available.
acceptance_test_for_future: A candidate that improves prose but loses source grounding fails gate and stays proposed, not accepted.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: Claw-ED README.md:77 and clawed/agent_core/tools/base.py:19-35 define read_only/write_local/network_call/package_install risk levels and approval defaults.
problem_it_solves: Teacher-facing systems need explicit approval boundaries for local writes, network calls, and export actions.
input: Action request, artifact manifest, risk level, teacher review state, target surface.
stage_owner: Teacher review gate and future R95/R210 action policy.
human_review_point: Teacher confirms write/export/network actions; read-only previews remain allowed.
do_not_adopt: Do not import Claw-ED approval DB, tool registry, standing approval behavior, or local file policy.
risk: Standing approvals can normalize high-risk actions if copied without Xiaobei-specific consent policy.
preconditions_before_use: Xiaobei action taxonomy must distinguish preview, candidate patch, accepted preview, formal apply, export, provider call, and external publish.
acceptance_test_for_future: Attempting R95 export without accepted teacher review returns blocked/export_not_eligible with no file written.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: README.md:24-28 and .github/agents/course-planner.md:7-11 say course-planner designs course outline/metadata and is out of scope for lesson-level content.
problem_it_solves: Course/unit planning and lesson body writing become tangled if the same stage owns both.
input: Course title, grade, subject, lesson count, duration, unit progression requirements.
output_artifact: unit_or_course_metadata with lesson index, progression, and handoff targets.
stage_owner: Future R240 free-prep intake or unit planner.
human_review_point: Teacher confirms course/unit metadata before single lesson generation.
do_not_adopt: Do not copy saniales GPL-3.0 agent files, prompt wording, scripts, templates, or directory structure.
risk: If course metadata writes lesson body, source policy and teacher review become blurred.
preconditions_before_use: R240 must define whether request is free prep, uploaded lesson repair, or unit planning.
acceptance_test_for_future: Unit planner output contains no lesson body paragraphs and only declares metadata/handoff fields.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: docs/WORKFLOW.md:7-18 and README.md:69-72 describe highlights, discourse, slides, and export as staged artifacts.
problem_it_solves: Later stages need stable artifacts to consume instead of implicit chat context.
input: Confirmed metadata, source ledger, teacher intent, lesson number/topic.
output_artifact: lesson_handoff_artifact containing purpose, accepted inputs, pending questions, artifact owner, and next-stage instruction.
stage_owner: R240 to R201, and R201 to R252 handoff.
human_review_point: Teacher can see what is known, missing, and being handed off.
do_not_adopt: Do not use saniales directory names or template files as-is.
risk: A handoff without missing-field markers encourages invented lesson content.
preconditions_before_use: Missing source fields and low-confidence fields must be represented explicitly.
acceptance_test_for_future: Every handoff has input_source, output_target, allowed_writer, missing_fields, and no hidden chat-only dependency.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: .github/agents/slides-maker.md:25-42 creates slides from existing highlights/discourse and treats templates as layout guides, not fixed counts.
problem_it_solves: Slides and big-screen pages should be derived from accepted lesson/timeline artifacts, not from a separate free-generation prompt.
input: Accepted lesson sections, classroom_timeline, teacher_execution_map, page constraints.
output_artifact: big_screen_page_plan or slide_storyboard with page refs and source dependencies.
human_review_point: Teacher previews page plan before PPT/export generation.
do_not_adopt: Do not copy MARP workflow or scripts; do not force external template counts.
risk: Slide generation before timeline contract can create display pages that do not match classroom pacing.
preconditions_before_use: R252 teacher_execution_map and classroom_timeline contracts exist.
acceptance_test_for_future: Each big-screen page references a timeline step and accepted lesson section; orphan pages fail validation.

source_evidence: R250 agent workflow reference states the right abstraction is one stage owning one artifact boundary; saniales separates course planner, moderator, lesson planner, slides maker, and export.
problem_it_solves: Multi-agent or multi-stage systems drift when several stages can write the same artifact.
input: Stage ID, target artifact, write permission, source inputs.
output_artifact: artifact_owner_registry mapping artifact to one owner and allowed readers.
human_review_point: Review package states which stage owns each artifact before implementation.
do_not_adopt: Do not adopt saniales implementation; adopt the independent principle only.
risk: Without ownership, R200/R201/R220/R240 can overwrite each other or leak candidate text into teacher main view.
preconditions_before_use: Each stage must declare owner, output artifact, and non-goals.
acceptance_test_for_future: A validator fails if two active stages claim write ownership over the same artifact.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: classmin README.md:45-54 describes classroom timeline and time system; timeline_parser.py:2-69 parses timeline into stages and steps.
problem_it_solves: A lesson design cannot become classroom execution without time-bounded steps.
input: Accepted single_lesson_template process episodes, teacher intent, duration_minutes.
output_artifact: classroom_timeline with stage_id, stage_name, start_sec, end_sec, teacher_action, student_action, purpose, source_ref.
stage_owner: Future R252 teacher execution map and classroom timeline contract.
human_review_point: Teacher confirms pacing and stage order before classroom support.
do_not_adopt: Do not adopt classmin parser or runtime; define Xiaobei-native schema.
risk: Generated time plans can look precise while lacking source or feasibility grounding.
preconditions_before_use: single_lesson_template has stable process_episodes and duration.
acceptance_test_for_future: Timeline total duration matches lesson duration tolerance and every step maps to a source or teacher-confirmed gap.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: classmin README.md:62 and web/pages/simulate.py lines around PPT page parsing describe teacher [PPT页] references.
problem_it_solves: Teacher execution and big-screen display need a shared reference rather than separate flows.
input: classroom_timeline step, page plan, accepted lesson section.
output_artifact: big_screen_page_reference_contract with page_id, timeline_step_id, display_purpose, teacher_cue, source_ref.
stage_owner: Future R230 surface contract, read by R252 and R210.
human_review_point: Teacher reviews page sequence and cues before display/export.
do_not_adopt: Do not adopt classmin PPT markers literally as UI/runtime syntax.
risk: If page refs are free text only, Xiaojiao cannot safely reason about display state.
preconditions_before_use: R230 has page ID semantics and R252 timeline step IDs.
acceptance_test_for_future: Every page reference resolves to one timeline step; unresolved page IDs fail gate.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: classmin README.md:53-54 describes fixed context, time budget, teacher [用时], batch timing, and stage advancement.
problem_it_solves: Xiaojiao needs pacing signals that help the teacher run class without rewriting lesson content.
input: classroom_timeline, elapsed time, current stage, teacher action status.
output_artifact: pacing_signal with on_track, over_time, under_time, next_step_hint, teacher_confirm_required.
stage_owner: Future R210 in-class support, after R252 contract.
human_review_point: Teacher receives pacing suggestion and chooses whether to act.
do_not_adopt: Do not adopt classmin simulation loop or automatic stage advancement into live classroom mode.
risk: Automatic pacing control can overstep teacher authority.
preconditions_before_use: Timeline is teacher-confirmed and Xiaojiao action policy distinguishes suggestion from apply.
acceptance_test_for_future: Pacing output is advisory only and never mutates template, timeline, or big-screen page plan.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: classmin README.md:63 and state_tracker.py:73-75 track understanding, attention, and frustration with Python rules.
problem_it_solves: A rehearsal simulator can reveal whether a lesson is likely too hard, too long, or low-engagement.
input: classroom_timeline, activity type, simulated student profile preset, teacher action sequence.
output_artifact: simulation_diagnostic only, with understanding/attention/frustration as fictional rehearsal metrics.
stage_owner: Future R250/R252 simulation planning, not live student analytics.
human_review_point: Teacher reviews simulation warnings as planning hints.
do_not_adopt: Do not persist simulated state as real student profile; do not connect to actual student records.
risk: Simulated learner state can be mistaken for real学情 evidence.
preconditions_before_use: UI and data model label this as synthetic rehearsal only.
acceptance_test_for_future: Validator blocks any simulated metric from entering student archive, source ledger, or teacher-main evidence.

source_evidence: R250 classroom reference maps classmin's time budget, activity type, PPT page references, and student-state simulation to future Xiaojiao in-class support.
problem_it_solves: Xiaojiao needs a classroom role that supports teacher execution without becoming an autonomous lesson writer.
output_artifact: xiaojiao_in_class_support_boundary with allowed suggestions and forbidden writes.
human_review_point: Teacher confirms suggestions; Xiaojiao cannot auto-apply lesson or page changes.
do_not_adopt: Do not connect classmin CrewAI/AutoGen/Chroma/Ollama stack or simulation engine.
risk: In-class assistant can overstep into controlling display or modifying plan if action policy is vague.
preconditions_before_use: R210 action policy and R230/R252 artifact contracts exist.
acceptance_test_for_future: Xiaojiao can suggest next step or pacing warning, but any write/export/display switch requires explicit teacher action.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: README.md:60-65 and app.py:55-62 list lesson title, subject, grade, duration, key vocabulary, and supporting materials.
problem_it_solves: Free prep needs a small entry form that does not overwhelm teachers.
input: lesson_title, subject, grade, duration_minutes, key_vocabulary, supporting_materials.
output_artifact: free_prep_intake_minimum with missing-field markers and generation_mode.
human_review_point: Teacher confirms minimum intake and missing fields before generation.
do_not_adopt: Do not adopt Divanshi Streamlit UI, direct provider call, old OpenAI SDK pattern, or hardcoded key handling.
risk: Minimum inputs can produce generic lessons unless missing source/standards are explicit.
preconditions_before_use: R240 must define baseline-only, candidate patch, free draft, and review-only modes.
acceptance_test_for_future: A free-prep request with only minimum fields creates intake and missing-field list, not a formal lesson body.

source_evidence: R250 free-prep reference extends minimum fields with textbook_version, unit_title, curriculum_standard_or_stage, teacher_intent, material_condition, and generation_mode.
problem_it_solves: Chinese primary art prep needs教材版本, 单元, 学段/课标, and teacher intent beyond generic lesson title/grade.
input: Minimum free-prep fields plus textbook_version, unit_title, curriculum_standard_or_stage, teacher_intent, material_condition, generation_mode.
stage_owner: Future R240B intake schema and workflow contract.
human_review_point: Teacher sees which China art context fields are missing or inferred.
do_not_adopt: Do not use external prompt wording or JSON shape directly.
risk: Without local extensions, free prep becomes subject-generic and weakly grounded.
preconditions_before_use: R240A audit identifies current free-prep entry points and existing schema gaps.
acceptance_test_for_future: R240 intake validator distinguishes required, recommended, optional, inferred, and teacher-confirmation fields.

source_evidence: R250 evidence map: app.py:6 has a hardcoded placeholder API key pattern; requirements.txt:1-2 pins streamlit==1.24.1 and openai==0.28.0.
problem_it_solves: Prevents a small demo from becoming a provider/security precedent.
stage_owner: Provider gate and future R240 planning.
human_review_point: Reviewer verifies free-prep implementation does not copy demo provider or UI approach.
do_not_adopt: Do not adopt Streamlit UI, direct API key entry, direct OpenAI call, old SDK dependency, or app.py structure.
risk: Demo-grade provider handling can leak secrets or bypass Xiaobei provider sandbox.
preconditions_before_use: Any provider call remains behind Xiaobei provider sandbox and candidate gate.
acceptance_test_for_future: R240 implementation review fails if UI collects raw API keys or calls provider directly.
